changes: Documentation site — full setup guide and documentation now live at diskovarr.com. Covers installation, all admin panel settings, every page, integrations, and the full API reference.
Documentation link in admin panel — a Documentation pill in the admin panel version strip links directly to the docs site, visible on all tabs.

Fix: Cast to TV device isolation — GDM (UDP broadcast) and PMS /clients removed as device discovery sources; both ran on the server’s local network, exposing the admin’s Plex players to all users. Device lookup now uses plex.tv/api/v2/resources and devices.xml with each user’s own token so every user sees only their own registered devices.

Fix: Cast to TV remote casting — playMedia was being sent with the server admin token, which cannot control another user’s Plex client. The command is now sent with the requesting user’s own token. Connection lookup also prefers relay connections (work from any network) over local LAN connections, fixing cast failures for users on a different network than the server.

Per-user default landing page — users can choose Diskovarr or Diskovarr Requests as their landing page in My Settings. Admins can set this per-user too. Removed the server-wide landing page setting from the admin panel.
Request count in admin user table — the Users tab now shows a Requests column with each user's total request count.

Admin users pagination — the Users tab paginates at 10 per page (configurable to 25 or 50 via a dropdown). The per-page selector is always visible and pagination loads in place without a full page reload.

Fix: Season limit bypass — service users with a per-season limit could bypass it because the cost was based on the explicit seasons list. Automated tools always send seasons:[1], so every show counted as 1 season. The limit now charges the full TMDB season count and stores it in the DB so the rolling window stays accurate.
Fix: Season bubbles overflowing on queue page — shows with more than 9 seasons selected now truncate to a ... ellipsis bubble followed by the last 8 seasons.

Fix: Experimental cast — GDM discovery now broadcasts to the Plex multicast address in addition to broadcast. The cast endpoint now correctly creates a PlayQueue before sending playMedia. Direct player connection lookup added as primary cast path before falling back to PMS relay.
Nav logo size — the SVG logo in the top-left nav bar is larger and more proportional to the Diskovarr wordmark.

Fix: Show/hide eye icons on API key fields — the toggle icon was invisible on connection fields that use empty <button> elements; all fields now show a consistent eye icon matching the Riven/Agregarr fields.

Connections tab cleanup — the Riven/DUMB and Agregarr instruction boxes have been moved into ⓘ info modals next to the section headers; the DUMB request mode selector is now positioned to the right of the API key field for a cleaner layout; DUMB and Agregarr API key fields are wider and easier to read.

Fix: Empty recommendation tag bubbles — items with low-confidence recently-released signals had a null reason label that rendered as a blank bubble on cards. Null and empty reasons are now filtered out.

API endpoint reference expanded — the reference table in Admin → General now documents all 28 public API endpoints across 6 categories: Recommendations & Discovery, Library & Search, Watchlist, Requests, Issues, and Notifications.

TMDB pipeline — region and language preferences (set in My Settings) are now applied to TMDB discover queries. Mature content toggle is stored server-side and controls TMDB include_adult at fetch time instead of only hiding cards client-side. Discover candidates are pre-fetched once per unique region+language+mature combination and shared across all matching users; per-user scoring runs every 28 minutes in the background so Explore loads instantly from a warm cache.

Shared TMDB detail cache — full item details (genres, keywords, cast, directors, ratings, trailers) are fetched and cached in the database once per item when the shared candidate pool is built. All users score against the same enriched data with no duplicate API calls. Per-user candidates from your watch history (TMDB recommendations, similar titles, person matches, keyword discovery, Plex related items) are still fetched per user and refreshed in the background.

Requested items in recommendations — items that have been requested but are not yet in the library now appear in recommendations with a Requested badge. If the item was requested by someone else, the request button becomes Notify Me — clicking it creates a follow entry and notifies you when the title becomes available.
Hide Requested toggle — a new toggle on the Explore page filters requested items out of all recommendation and trending sections server-side. Toggle state persists across sessions.

Accent-colored background gradient — all pages now show a subtle radial glow using the current accent color; spotlight from top center with a softer glow at the bottom. Updates in real-time when accent color is changed in the admin panel.
Fix: Default request service setting was not being passed to the frontend on explore and search pages, causing requests to always route to Overseerr regardless of admin settings.
Fix: Season rate limit bypass — the limit check now runs after deriving the real season count from TMDB so Agregarr requests for multi-season shows are counted correctly
Fix: Info modal credits username corrected to gage117
Fix: Approving, denying, editing, or deleting a queue request no longer scrolls to the top — all actions update the row in place
Fix: Resolving, closing, or deleting an issue no longer scrolls to the top — row is removed in place; delete uses the action modal instead of native confirm()
Fix: Queue items-per-page preference is now saved to localStorage and restored on page load
Fix: Queue and issues tables scroll horizontally on mobile instead of the whole page panning
Issues section added to the info modal
Bulk user settings — select multiple users in Admin → Users and apply the same settings override to all at once; each field has a three-state selector so only the fields you care about are changed
Season bubbles on TV requests — the request queue now shows individual season number chips (S1 S2 S3…) for each TV request
Request age — the queue Date column now shows relative age (e.g. 3 hours ago, 2 days ago) instead of an absolute date
Info modal credits — a Created by line at the bottom of the info modal links to the GitHub profiles of the project contributors

Fix: DUMB queue polling — approved requests now return media.status=3 (PROCESSING) so DUMB picks them up; added missing /movie/:id and /tv/:id detail endpoints DUMB uses for IMDb ID lookup; added type and media.media_type fields that DUMB accesses internally
Fix: Agregarr service user attribution — requests via the shim were attributed to the generic app user; fixed by reading the X-Api-User header Agregarr sends
Fix: Agregarr requests always pending — shim now calls getEffectiveAutoApprove() so requests auto-approve when the global or per-user setting allows it
Fix: Agregarr requests with non-TMDB IDs — requests whose TMDB ID returns 404 are now silently skipped with a clean 201 so Agregarr's sync continues
Fix: Agregarr rate limit response — hitting a per-user season or movie limit now returns a silent 201 instead of 403 so Agregarr's sync continues uninterrupted
Fix: Approve request with service=none — the approve endpoint now picks the best available service dynamically when service is none
Fix: Delete confirmation modal — replaced native confirm() with a custom in-page modal that supports a don't ask again checkbox backed by localStorage

DUMB request polling — DUMB connects to Diskovarr as its Overseerr source; enable in Admin → Connections → Riven → DUMB Integration; choose Pull (DUMB polls /api/v1/request) or Push (Diskovarr pushes to Riven on approval); DUMB marks content available via PUT /api/v1/media/:id/available
Search page autocomplete — TMDB suggestions on the search results page search bar, matching the nav bar behaviour
Fix: DUMB API key generated as 68 characters to satisfy Riven's Overseerr key length validation
Fix: Admin session cookie sameSite hardened from lax to strict
Riven/DUMB as a request service — Riven can now be selected as a request routing target alongside Overseerr, Radarr, and Sonarr; configure in Admin → Connections → Riven

Riven/DUMB torrent browser — DMM-style browser in Admin → Connections → Riven → Browse Torrents; search by title, view Torrentio results with RD cache status, inject chosen torrent into Riven in one click; includes season selector for TV and manual magnet paste fallback

Agregarr integration — Overseerr-compatible API shim at /api/v1/ lets Agregarr connect to Diskovarr as if it were Overseerr; enable in Admin → Connections → Agregarr; Agregarr creates service users automatically; their requests appear in the queue with a bot badge

Plex WebSocket — real-time library change notifications without Plex Pass; invalidates cache and checks fulfilled requests instantly when new content is added; auto-reconnects with exponential back-off
Fix: Admin connection tab eye/copy icon buttons now use solid filled icons, readable at all sizes

Plex webhook listener — Diskovarr now receives library.new events from Plex at POST /api/webhooks/plex, automatically invalidating the library cache and triggering fulfilled request checks when new content is added (requires Plex Pass)
Issue comments — users and admins can exchange comments on any issue in the issue detail modal; admin comments notify the reporter, user comments notify all admins
Discord independent webhook/bot toggles — both can now be enabled simultaneously, each with its own notification type list, embed poster toggle, and Save/Test button
Discord per-panel embed poster — webhook and bot each have their own embed poster toggle
Discord bot username and avatar settings moved into the Bot Token panel
Pushover notification sound — select from the full Pushover sound library or choose device default or silent
Pushover embed poster — attach the title's poster image to Pushover notifications
Pushover setup hints — help links to Pushover documentation under the API Token and User Key fields
Issue Comments notification type consolidated — the two separate issue comment checkboxes are merged into a single toggle across Discord and Pushover notification type lists
Admin broadcast notifications — type a message in Admin → Notifications and send it to all users at once via in-app bell, Discord, and Pushover simultaneously
Broadcast bell notifications open a modal showing the full message instead of navigating away
Last Visit column in admin Users tab — updates whenever a logged-in user browses the app, replacing the old Last Sync timestamp

Global requests disable toggle — the Request button is hidden site-wide and submissions are blocked when disabled; toggle is in Admin → Request Limits; individual users can be granted an override via per-user admin settings
Requests can now be submitted without a configured request service — the request appears in the queue and triggers notifications as normal
Landing page toggle: the Diskovarr Requests option is now disabled when the Requests tab is turned off

Request fulfilled notifications — when a requested title appears in the Plex library the requester receives a bell, Discord, and/or Pushover notification; also triggers instantly via Plex webhook (register in Plex → Settings → Webhooks)
Discord and Pushover notifications now group into one bundled message per event (matching the bell), with the poster from the first title embedded full-width
Discord and Pushover sends are skipped if the user already read the bell notification before the delivery window
Discord: issue_new and issue_updated now default to enabled for existing configs; embed poster defaults to on; issue_updated added to shared-channel webhook
Pushover: request_available type added to admin notification settings
Global nav search bar — always-visible search input on desktop between the nav tabs and bell; on mobile collapses to a magnifying glass icon that expands to a full-width overlay bar
Nav search shows TMDB autocomplete suggestions and navigates to the full search results page
Search results page: library items now show the full detail modal including Cast to TV and Report Issue; non-library items show the request modal
Back button on search page now returns to the previous page, or falls back to the configured landing page
Search results no longer hang when Plex TV library is slow — stale DB items are served immediately while a background sync runs
Elevated users can now view, approve, deny, edit, and delete all queue requests — not just their own
Changelog modal now shows only the three most recent versions

Issue reporting — report problems with library items directly from the detail modal; TV shows have a scope selector (Entire Series / Specific Season / Specific Episode) with season and episode number inputs
Issues page at /issues — users see their own reports with scope, status, and a Details button; admins and elevated users see all issues with poster, reporter, scope, status, and full detail view
Issue details modal — shows full description, scope breakdown, reporter (admin view), status, and any admin note
Admins can resolve or close issues with an optional note, displayed inline below the title and in the details modal
Notifications: issue_new sent to admins/elevated users when an issue is reported; issue_updated sent to the reporter when status changes; clicking either notification navigates to /issues
Fix: bell notifications now mark as read immediately when clicked, before navigating away
Discord and Pushover: issue_new and issue_updated added to notification type lists in admin settings
User settings and admin per-user settings: notify_issue_update (all users) and notify_issue_new (elevated users) toggles added
Issues link added to nav menu between Queue and Admin
Discord bot avatar upload — admins can upload a custom PNG/JPG/GIF (128×128 recommended, max 2 MB) for the bot's identity, with live preview; or enter a direct URL as an alternative
Discord/Pushover notification enable toggles — now rendered as styled slide-toggles at the top of each credential block in per-user admin settings
Owner lock — when a user is designated as the server owner, their elevated privileges toggle is locked, checked, and labelled in per-user admin settings
Recommendation pre-warming — after each library sync, user recommendation caches are rebuilt in the background so page loads are instant
Fix: recommendation pre-warm logger reference error corrected
Request queue page — users can view and manage their own requests; admins can review, approve, deny (with optional note), edit, or delete all requests
Per-user settings page — users can set region, language, notification preferences, and auto-request watchlist options
Admin per-user settings panel — full per-user controls for request limits, auto-approve overrides, elevated privileges, and notification preferences
Notification system — in-app bell with dropdown, Discord webhook (per-user and global), and Pushover push notifications
Elevated privileges role — admin-level flag per user with toggle in admin user panel
Auto-request from watchlist — global setting with per-user overrides for movies and TV shows
Changelog modal — version badge in the About panel opens a changelog history popup
Desktop user button redesigned — compact button matching the mobile FAB style with dropdown menu
Discord mode selector redesigned as a slide toggle (Webhook ↔ Bot Token) matching the site's toggle style
Bot mode shared-channel webhook option is now a toggle that reveals the URL field on demand
App Public URL setting moved to General Settings tab; enabling Discord bot mode without it set shows an error
Discord Server Invite Link — admins can set a non-expiring invite; users see a 'Join Server' prompt in their settings
Notification bell shows recent read notifications greyed out below unread; clicking 'Request available' navigates to the queue
Privileged user routing — elevated users receive admin-type notifications in addition to Plex admins
request_process_failed notification type added to Discord and Pushover agent type lists
Quality profile names persisted — saved profile name shown in the dropdown if Radarr/Sonarr is temporarily unreachable
Per-user admin settings page — action buttons for Re-sync Watched, Clear Watched, Clear Dismissals, Clear Requests
Themed checkboxes now consistent site-wide
